intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses consensus AI (Claude + GPT-4o) to autonomously generate, test, and ship software from TRD specifications. The system encompasses 16 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS application shell with Touch ID/Keychain/XPC (TRD-1), dual-model consensus engine with parallel generation and Claude arbitration (TRD-2), confidence-gated build pipeline with 20-pass fix loops and pr_type routing (TRD-3), multi-agent coordination via GitHub-stored build ledger (TRD-4), GitHub integration with rate limiting and CI workflow management (TRD-5), five-lens holistic code review (TRD-6), adaptive TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes (TRD-7), full SwiftUI design system with health dashboard and remediation stream (TRD-8), self-hosted Mac CI runner infrastructure (TRD-9), FAISS-backed document store with injection defense (TRD-10), comprehensive security threat model (TRD-11), backend startup/shutdown sequence with version handshake (TRD-12), three-layer recovery and state management with build memory and build rules (TRD-13), CI false-positive hardening and conftest.py dual-layer import fix (TRD-14), operational runbook (TRD-15), testing strategy with FM taxonomy and 35-test regression contract (TRD-16), and self-healing autonomous observability and remediation loop (TRD-17). Cloud deployment APIs, Figma design-to-code pipeline, and CraftedConnect SDK (referenced in TRD-8 §12 and TRD-17 §5) will be resolved during PRD decomposition per operator direction.

Clarifications:
  - Create them as part of the PRD process using best practices
  - correct
  - Create them as part of the PRD process using best practices
  - Create them as part of the PRD process using best practices
  - those should be configurable aspects of the UI, so that they can be entered during onboarding

Operator on 'Should the initial build prioritize the Python backend (cons':
parallel from the start. Build the PRDs in order that allows for maximum code deployment efficiency. Ensure that each PR follows this guidance Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs.

Operator on 'For the CraftedConnect SDK (TRD-18 merged into TRD-17): shou':
Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.

Operator on 'TRD-1 §7.1 states v1 is NOT sandboxed. Should the build targ':
correct

Operator on 'The 35-test regression suite (TRD-16 §11) is specified as a ':
the are recommendations for tests, not explicit tests

prd_plan.title: Monorepo Foundation, Configuration Baseline, and Architecture Contracts
Swift macOS Shell Core: App Lifecycle, State Model, and Onboarding
Swift Security Layer: Touch ID, Session Control, and Keychain Management
XPC Bridge: Unix Socket Transport, Nonce Handshake, and Message Protocol
Backend Process Management: Launch, Health Monitoring, and Restart Policy
Python Backend Runtime: Startup Sequence, Command Router, and Shutdown Guarantees
GitHub Integration: Repository Operations, Rate Limiting, and CI Workflow Management
Document Store: FAISS-Backed Ingestion, Retrieval, and Injection Defense
Consensus Engine: Dual-Provider Parallel Generation and Claude Arbitration
Recovery, State Persistence, Build Memory, and Build Rules
Confidence-Gated Build Pipeline Orchestrator
PRD Planner, PR Planner, and Decomposition System
Per-PR Code Generation Pipeline: Repo Context, Self-Correction, and Lint Gate
Failure Handler, Fix Loop, and CI Feedback Remediation
Multi-Agent Coordination and GitHub-Stored Build Ledger
Holistic Code Review: Five-Lens Analysis, Report Generation, and Fix PRs
Adaptive TRD Development Workflow: Founder, Engineer, and Consultant Modes
SwiftUI Design System: Tokens, Components, and Reusable Primitives
Build Stream UI: Intent Bar, Stream Cards, Gate Cards, and Context Panels
Health Dashboard UI: Target Cards, Diagnosis Views, and Remediation Stream
TRD Session UI: Mode-Adaptive Views, Meeting Timer, and Deployment Visuals
Figma Import, Sketch Interpretation, and Design-to-Code Pipeline
Cross-Cutting Security Enforcement and Threat Controls
Self-Hosted macOS CI Runner Infrastructure and Swift Validation Pipeline
CI False-Positive Hardening and Import Path Resolution
Testing Strategy, Regression Contract, and FM Taxonomy Enforcement
Operational Runbook Integration and Operator Recovery Surfaces
Autonomous Observability, Detection, and Self-Healing Remediation Loop
CraftedConnect SDK: Target Registration, Health Reporting, and In-App Issue Submission
Cloud Deployment API Integration and Deployment Target Orchestration

1.title: Add monorepo directory skeleton and root sentinel files
1.summary: Creates the canonical repository directory layout defined in FR-1: forge-docs/, forge-standards/, src/, tests/, Crafted/, CraftedTests/, .github/workflows/, scripts/, schemas/ with sentinel files (.gitkeep or __init__.py). Includes root-level VERSION (38.153.0), LICENSE, and .gitignore. No logic, no configuration content — skeleton only.

2.title: Add remaining scaffold directories and root conftest
2.summary: Creates remaining directories from FR-1 that exceed PR-1's file limit: tests/, Crafted/, CraftedTests/, .github/workflows/ with sentinel files, plus root conftest.py with sys.path injection for pytest discovery.

3.title: Add pyproject.toml and requirements.txt
3.summary: Creates pyproject.toml with project metadata, Python >=3.11 requirement, version field matching VERSION file (38.153.0), pytest configuration with pythonpath=['src'], and tool sections for ruff/mypy. Creates requirements.txt with pinned Python dependencies including pyyaml and jsonschema.

4.title: Add architecture standards documents
4.summary: Creates the four canonical standards documents in forge-standards/: ARCHITECTURE.md (two-process Swift shell + Python backend, XPC boundary, process lifecycle), INTERFACES.md (XPC message contract, version handshake protocol, health-check schema), CONVENTIONS.md (naming rules, PR sizing, import ordering, error taxonomy), DECISIONS.md (ADR log template with first entry for two-process architecture choice).

5.title: Add build rules and AGENTS.md standards
5.summary: Creates forge-standards/build_rules.md defining confidence gates, 20-pass fix loop parameters, pr_type routing rules, and build ledger schema contract. Creates AGENTS.md at repo root defining agent behavior contracts: directory ownership, file modification rules, commit message format, and canonical directory-to-TRD mapping table.

6.title: Add TRD-to-workstream decomposition map
6.summary: Creates forge-docs/PRD_DECOMPOSITION_MAP.md mapping each TRD (1-17) to its implementation workstream(s), owning PRDs, directory scope, and dependency graph. Includes the deferred-scope table for cloud deployment APIs, Figma pipeline, and CraftedConnect SDK.

7.title: Add Makefile and bootstrap script
7.summary: Creates scripts/bootstrap.sh that validates Python >=3.11, creates venv, installs requirements.txt, verifies Xcode CLI tools, and runs version-sync check. Creates Makefile with targets: bootstrap, lint, test, build, clean, version-check. Bootstrap script is the single entry point for new developer onboarding.

8.title: Add unified configuration JSON schema
8.summary: Creates schemas/config.schema.json — the JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) defining the unified configuration contract: all operator-configurable onboarding fields (github_token, openai_api_key, anthropic_api_key, ci_runner_label, cloud_deployment_target, figma_api_key, crafted_connect_endpoint), each with type, default value, validation rules (regex patterns, length bounds), and persistence target annotation (keychain | user_defaults | runtime_only).

9.title: Add XPC message and version handshake JSON schemas
9.summary: Creates schemas/xpc_message.schema.json defining the XPC message envelope (msg_id, msg_type enum, timestamp, payload, version), and schemas/version_handshake.schema.json defining the startup handshake contract (shell_version, backend_version, protocol_version, compatible boolean). Both enforce INTERFACES.md contracts as machine-readable validation.

10.title: Add backend path constants module
10.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/__init__.py and src/forge_agent/paths.py defining canonical repository and subsystem path constants (REPO_ROOT, SRC_DIR, SCHEMAS_DIR, etc.) used by config loading, security enforcement, and bootstrap code.

11.title: Add version sync utilities and validation
11.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/version.py that reads VERSION file, parses semver, exposes get_version() and check_version_sync() (validates VERSION matches pyproject.toml version field). Creates scripts/version_check.sh invoked by Makefile target and CI. Enforces the single-source-of-truth version contract from FR-1.

12.title: Add path security module with traversal defense
12.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/security/ package with paths.py implementing path validation: resolves symlinks, rejects traversal (../ sequences), enforces an allowlist of writable directories, and raises SecurityViolation on any attempt to write outside boundaries. Includes exceptions.py with SecurityViolation definition.

13.title: Add configuration dataclasses and types
13.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/types.py defining typed Python configuration models: ConfigSnapshot frozen dataclass, PersistenceTarget enum (keychain, user_defaults, runtime_only), field metadata annotations, and onboarding field definitions. This module has no I/O — pure data definitions.

14.title: Add configuration schema validator
14.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/schema_validator.py that validates configuration dictionaries against schemas/config.schema.json using jsonschema, produces actionable error messages with field names, and enforces persistence-target annotation rules (refuses to log keychain-targeted values).

15.title: Add configuration loader
15.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/loader.py that reads configuration from environment variables and optional YAML/JSON files, invokes schema validation, applies defaults for missing optional fields, and returns a frozen ConfigSnapshot dataclass. Integrates with path constants for schema file resolution.

16.title: Add Swift configuration contract types
16.summary: Defines Swift-side Codable models for onboarding settings, persistence targets, version metadata, and backend handshake payloads. These mirror the Python types and JSON schemas to enforce cross-boundary contract alignment.

17.title: Add Swift configuration defaults, validation, and persistence routing
17.summary: Implements Swift-side defaulting and field validation for operator-configurable onboarding values. Defines PersistenceScope enum and routing rules that classify fields into UserDefaults, Keychain, or runtime-only scopes.

18.title: Add backend bootstrap prerequisite checker
18.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/bootstrap/ package with prereq_check.py that validates required repository files exist, environment prerequisites are met (Python version, venv active), foundational layout integrity is intact, and configuration can be loaded. Uses path constants and security module for safe file access.

19.title: Add CI workflow for Python lint, type-check, and test
19.summary: Creates .github/workflows/python-ci.yml: triggers on push/PR, sets up Python 3.11, installs requirements.txt, runs ruff check, mypy with strict mode on src/, pytest with coverage reporting. Validates VERSION sync via scripts/version_check.sh.

20.title: Tests for scaffold integrity and directory structure
20.summary: Programmatically validates the monorepo scaffold: asserts all FR-1 directories exist, all sentinel files are present, VERSION file contains valid semver, conftest.py contains sys.path injection, and .gitignore covers Python/Swift/macOS patterns.

21.title: Tests for JSON schema validity and cross-schema consistency
21.summary: Validates all JSON schemas in schemas/ are valid JSON Schema draft 2020-12, checks config.schema.json contains all required onboarding fields with persistence annotations, validates xpc_message.schema.json envelope structure, and verifies version_handshake.schema.json protocol_version field exists.

22.title: Tests for configuration loader security boundaries
22.summary: Dedicated security test suite: verifies keychain-targeted fields are never logged or serialized to disk, validates malformed config raises ValidationError not KeyError, confirms environment variable injection with special characters is safely handled, and tests missing required fields produce actionable error messages.

23.title: Tests for path security traversal defense
23.summary: Dedicated security test suite: tests symlink resolution blocking, ../ traversal rejection, allowlist enforcement for each permitted directory, rejection of writes to non-allowlisted paths (e.g., /etc/, ~/.ssh/), and SecurityViolation exception semantics. Includes parametrized attack vector tests.

24.title: Tests for cross-boundary contract alignment
24.summary: Validates Python configuration types and JSON schemas stay aligned: asserts ConfigSnapshot fields match config.schema.json required properties, PersistenceTarget enum values match schema annotations, and version contract fields match handshake schema. Guards against schema-code drift.
